wedges.name: Industrial Robot Self-Calibration Module
wedges.tagline: Autonomous calibration system that teaches industrial robot arms to self-tune to factory stations in 30 minutes instead of 8 hours of human engineering.
wedges.mutation: seed: direct B2B application of Pong Academy's core loop to industrial automation
wedges.icp: Automation Engineers at mid-sized manufacturers ($50M-$500M revenue) running 10-100 robot cells who lose 2-4 production hours per week to recalibration after fixture changes, lighting shifts, or vibration drift
wedges.problem: Robot arms lose precision after environmental changes (lighting, vibration, 2mm fixture shifts) requiring expensive human recalibration that stops production for 4-8 hours per incident, happening 2-3x per month per cell
wedges.why_now: Manufacturing reshoring + labor shortage + AI perception breakthrough makes autonomous calibration economically critical; perception models now reliable enough for industrial precision
wedges.demand_evidence: a16z-AD-factory 'Renaissance of the American factory' specifically calls for AI+autonomy to speed design cycles with design-for-manufacture and deploy autonomy for precision tasks
wedges.mvp_plan: Partner with 2-3 manufacturers to install Pong Academy's perception+servo loop on existing robot cells; demonstrate 30-minute autonomous recalibration vs 4-8 hour human process; measure precision recovery and uptime improvement
wedges.market_expansion: $200M beachhead (10K+ robot cells in US mid-market manufacturing) expanding to $2B+ (robotic welding, assembly, packaging across automotive, electronics, food processing globally)
wedges.defensibility: High-signal episode dataset per factory environment + learned calibration policies create compound advantage; each deployment improves the universal calibration model while building customer-specific optimization moats
wedges.business_model: Manufacturing automation integrators pay $15K-$25K per robot cell for calibration module + $2K annual software subscription; 60% gross margin; sell through existing automation partners who have customer relationships
wedges.experiment_4wk: Install prototype on 3 partner robot cells, demonstrate autonomous recalibration after simulated environmental changes, measure precision recovery time vs manual baseline
wedges.go_no_go: >=30% faster calibration time with >=95% precision recovery on partner rigs in <=300 episodes
wedges.why_pitch_antler: Perfect founder-market fit: Ed's robotics perception expertise from L5 Automation + Saronic directly applies, while Aleh's enterprise B2B sales record (AB InBev, CDEK) maps to automation buyer motion. Clear $200M+ beachhead with defensible episode data flywheel.

qual.founder_fit_justification: Ed has direct robotics research engineering at L5 Automation, KEF Robotics, and NASA JPL with real-world perception expertise. Aleh closed AB InBev strategic investment and has enterprise B2B track record with technical products.
qual.rfp_which: a16z-AD-factory Renaissance of the American factory
qual.problem_intensity_justification: Manufacturing loses production time to robot recalibration multiple times per month - this is budgeted downtime with clear ROI calculation

wedges.name: OrchardBot Calibration Loop
wedges.tagline: Self-tuning actuation calibration for agricultural robots that adapt to each farm's unique conditions in real-time.
wedges.mutation: seed: precision-ag actuation calibration for weed-zapping/fruit-picking robots per orchard
wedges.icp: Head of Agricultural Operations at mid-scale farms (500-2000 acres) running robotic weed control or selective harvesting systems, triggered by poor robot success rates in new field conditions, controls $200K+ annual equipment budgets
wedges.problem: Agricultural robots achieve 60-80% success rates in lab conditions but drop to 30-50% in real orchards due to lighting variations, plant growth differences, and terrain irregularities - requiring expensive manual recalibration by robotics engineers for each new field deployment
wedges.why_now: Precision agriculture robots are finally cost-effective enough for mid-scale deployment, but the sim-to-real gap is the last bottleneck preventing widespread adoption beyond research farms
wedges.demand_evidence: YC-S26-1 AI for Low-Pesticide Agriculture: Garry Tan specifically calls out strawberries as $3B market in California alone with 60% labor costs, targeting 90% pesticide reduction through AI+robotics treating individual plants
wedges.mvp_plan: Partner with 2-3 farms already using robotic weed zappers or fruit pickers; deploy Pong Academy's calibration loop on their existing rigs to auto-tune actuation timing and camera angles over 100-300 field episodes per deployment
wedges.market_expansion: $200M beachhead in robotic weed control systems expanding to $2B+ precision agriculture market including selective harvesting, targeted spraying, and pruning automation across all specialty crops
wedges.defensibility: Farm-specific calibration datasets become proprietary per deployment; the autoresearch loop discovers optimal policies for each orchard's unique conditions that competitors cannot replicate without running the same episode count
wedges.business_model: Robotic OEMs pay $15-25K annual software license per deployed robot; 40% gross margins on SaaS model; acquire customers through direct partnerships with existing ag-robotics manufacturers; expand via multi-robot fleet management
wedges.experiment_4wk: Deploy calibration loop on 2 partner farms' existing weed-zapping robots, measure success rate improvement over 200 episodes per farm versus baseline manual calibration
wedges.go_no_go: >=30% relative success-rate improvement on partner rigs within 300 episodes compared to manual calibration baseline
wedges.why_pitch_antler: Precision agriculture is a proven $15B+ market with clear robotic adoption momentum but constrained by calibration bottlenecks that manual engineering cannot scale to solve. The autoresearch approach directly addresses the core blocker preventing ag-robotics from reaching mainstream farm deployment.

qual.founder_fit_justification: Ed has robotics research engineering at NASA JPL and perception expertise at defense/automation companies, but neither founder has direct agricultural domain experience or farm sales channels
qual.problem_intensity_justification: Implied by YC RFP demand for 90% pesticide reduction through robotic individual plant treatment, but no direct evidence of current calibration budgets

scores.band_projected: Potentially fundable; needs sharper proof or story

wedges.name: LabBot Precision Calibration Loop
wedges.tagline: Robotic lab instruments self-tune per protocol for lights-out operation.
wedges.mutation: seed: a16z autonomous labs RFP - direct autoresearch application
wedges.icp: Lab automation engineers at pharma/biotech running 24/7 robotic protocols with >$500K automation budgets
wedges.problem: Robotic pipettes/dispensers drift overnight requiring manual recalibration every 8-12 hours, blocking lights-out operation and wasting $200K+ annual tech time
wedges.why_now: AI lab protocols now generate thousands of experiments daily requiring 24/7 robotic execution; manual calibration is the bottleneck to full automation
wedges.demand_evidence: a16z-AD-autonomous-labs: 'As robotic manipulation improves, lights-out labs close the loop from hypothesis to experiment execution. DIRECTLY relevant to an autoresearch product.'
wedges.mvp_plan: Partner with 2-3 pharma labs: instrument their existing liquid handlers with vision feedback, run 100-episode calibration loops on pipette accuracy, demonstrate 8x longer uptime between manual interventions
wedges.market_expansion: $300M beachhead (lab automation at top 500 pharma/biotech); $2B+ expansion (academic research, clinical labs, food testing, semiconductor fabs)
wedges.defensibility: Episode dataset of instrument-specific drift patterns + calibration policies per lab environment creates data moat; workflow integration makes switching costly
wedges.business_model: Lab pays $50K annual license per robotic workstation + $10K setup; 80% gross margin; sell through existing lab automation VARs; expand to multi-instrument orchestration
wedges.experiment_4wk: Partner with 1 pharma lab, instrument their Hamilton liquid handler, run 200 calibration episodes, measure uptime extension vs baseline
wedges.go_no_go: >=4x uptime extension (32+ hour continuous operation vs 8-hour baseline) in <=200 episodes
wedges.why_pitch_antler: Direct match to a16z autonomous labs thesis with immediate pharma demand. Clear technical proof point unlocks massive lab efficiency gains.

qual.founder_fit_justification: Ed: robotics research engineering at NASA JPL + perception systems. Aleh: enterprise B2B closes (AB InBev) but lacks pharma/lab sales experience
qual.problem_intensity_justification: Lab automation engineers already budget $500K+ for robotic systems; manual calibration every 8-12 hours is documented operational pain blocking lights-out operation

scores.band_projected: Potentially fundable; needs sharper proof or story

wedges.name: Interceptor Aim Calibration Loop
wedges.tagline: Counter-drone interceptors self-tune aim per site's sensor rig geometry in ~100 episodes vs months of human calibration.
wedges.mutation: seed: YC S26 Counter-Swarm Defense RFP + Ed's Saronic defense perception background
wedges.icp: Defense contractor integration engineers setting up counter-drone systems at forward bases/critical infrastructure where $3M Patriot vs $500 FPV economics demand autonomous interceptors
wedges.problem: Each interceptor deployment requires weeks of manual sensor-fusion calibration to account for site-specific mount angles, vibration, interference patterns, and environmental factors - human engineering bottleneck blocks rapid deployment
wedges.why_now: FPV drone swarm tactics proven in Ukraine; DoD shifting from expensive missiles to autonomous interceptor networks; sensor fusion hardware commoditized but site calibration remains manual engineering nightmare
wedges.demand_evidence: YC-S26-5 Counter-Swarm Defense: Patriot $3M vs FPV drone $500 cost asymmetry driving need for autonomous interceptor systems with sensor-fusion and attacks on autonomy stack
wedges.mvp_plan: Mock interceptor rig with camera + projectile launcher; demonstrate 30-second setup vs 2-week traditional calibration by auto-learning site geometry through scored intercept episodes; validate with defense contractor on non-classified test range
wedges.market_expansion: $500M beachhead (counter-drone systems for 1000+ critical infrastructure sites); $5B+ expansion (autonomous defense systems, maritime interdiction, space debris tracking, precision munitions)
wedges.defensibility: Site-specific calibration dataset + learned sensor-fusion policies per deployment environment; each installation generates proprietary episode data that improves the loop's adaptation speed
wedges.business_model: Defense contractors pay $50K-200K per site license; 70% gross margins on software; CAC through defense industry conferences + Ed's Saronic network; expansion via additional threat vectors per site
wedges.experiment_4wk: Build interceptor simulator, partner with one defense contractor for non-classified testing, demonstrate measurable aim improvement vs baseline manual calibration on their test range
wedges.go_no_go: >=30% faster calibration convergence in <=100 episodes vs contractor's current manual process
wedges.why_pitch_antler: Defense spending surge + proven team (Ed's perception at Saronic + Aleh's enterprise sales track) + measurable technical wedge. Clear path from interceptor calibration to broader autonomous defense systems market.

qual.founder_fit_justification: Ed: Perception Engineer at Saronic (autonomous maritime/defense) + NASA JPL robotics = direct defense sensor-fusion background. Aleh: closes first enterprise customers cold (AB InBev) + quantified PMF discipline
qual.problem_intensity_justification: YC-S26-5 cites $3M Patriot vs $500 FPV drone cost asymmetry demonstrating urgent defense spending need

scores.band_projected: Potentially fundable; needs sharper proof or story

qual.founder_fit_justification: Ed: 'Robotics Research Engineer at L5 Automation (2021-2024)', 'Core expertise: real-world perception, sensor fusion, robotics research engineering' - direct industrial robotics experience. Aleh: 'AB InBev: closed strategic investment + global supply agreement', 'Validated unit economics $0.42/lb COGS, $3.35M CapEx at commercial scale' - proven manufacturing B2B sales and unit economics validation
qual.rfp_which: a16z-AD-factory: Renaissance of the American factory
qual.problem_intensity_justification: Manufacturers demonstrably spend $500K+ annually on commissioning engineering - this is budgeted, repeated, urgent production bottleneck

top3_selection: best wedge per normalized family, ranked by projected score, so the Top 3 are 3 genuinely distinct wedges
